English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Robert Matyas papers
Matyas, Robert M.
Katz, Robert D.
Williams, John I.
Young, Carleton S.
43-1-3368
7.6 cubic feet. 7.6 cubic feet.
1951-2012. Photographs, correspondence, publications Slides, photographs, and papers concering Matyas' career in facilities at Cornell, including his work on the Superconducting Super Collider, the Laboratory of Nuclear Studies, the Wilson Synchrotron Laboratory, Arecibo Ionospheric Observatory, and various other campus buildings. Collection material in English 5128048
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Maria Keating
Date completed:
August 2004
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez, Sept. 2004 Julia Parker, Oct. 2009
Date modified:
Jude Corina, January 2015
Cite As:
Robert Matyas papers, #43-01-3368. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Vice President for Facilities and Business Operations, Cornell University. Cornell University Class of 1951, Robert Matyas received a degree in architecture in 1952.
Scope and content
Slides, photographs, and papers concerning Matyas' career in facilities at Cornell, including his work on the Superconducting Super Collider, the Laboratory of Nuclear Studies, the Wilson Synchrotron Laboratory, Arecibo Ionospheric Observatory, Snee Hall (Geology), the Johnson Art Museum, and various other campus buildings. Also, Day Hall space planning documents, 1969-1972 and a report of the "Status of Old Country Club Land in Cayuga Heights," compiled by Thomas Mackesey, 1974. Includes programs for dedications of various campus buildings.
Scope and content
Also, Arecibo photographs by Dale Corson, photographs of Jason Seley sculptures, photographs of the Uris Library addition, other Arecibo and campus scenes, including glass negatives of the Arts Quad.
Scope and content
Also, "Living units for men, Cornell University," 1951, architecture student project report about dormitories at Cornell, by Robert D. Katz, Robert M. Matyas, John I. Williams, and Carlton S. Young.
Scope and content
"Some Things I Remember about the Anabel Taylor Interfaith Chapel at Cornell University," 2012
Related Materials
Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/40538
Slides (photographs). Photographs. Dormitories -- Designs and plans. Superconducting Super Collider. Cornell University -- : Buildings. Arecibo Ionospheric Observatory Cornell University. Laboratory of Nuclear Studies Wilson Synchrotron Laboratory (Cornell University) Cornell University. College of Architecture, Art, and Planning Cornell University -- : Student housing.

Scope and Contents
Harry Burgess, Wisconsin Trip, Tunnel, Isle of Shoals (1980), Commencement (1981), Manchester, NH (1981), Cape Cod (1981), Performing Arts Center, Observatory, Bill Hamilton, Arecibo Main Cable Inspection, Cornell Club in NYC, Toronto, Cornell University Medical College, Cornell University Heating Plant, Ithaca Gun, Cornell University Football, World Trade Center (Feb. 1987), New York City, California (1987), Chicago, Atlanta (Feb. 1987), Baker Laboratory, Washington, DC, Uris Library Addition, Cornell University Construction, Mining, Massachusetts Avenue House in Washington, DC, Geology Building Construction, Sab Micron Lab, Day Hall, VCAT, Sibley Hall, Mrs. Robert Purcell, St. Lawrence River, Pew Quad Dedication (1980), Cornell University Career Center, Statler West, Risley Hall, East Avenue, Nick Noyes, Indianapolis, Boston, assorted Cornell University views, assorted Arecibo views, and miscellaneous shots as well as slides of the following: New York City, Cornell University Construction, Performing Arts Center, Statler, Cornell University Medical College, Olin Library Construction, Uris Library, assorted Cornell University views, assorted Arecibo views, and assorted Ithaca viewsQuill & Dagger Alumni Association41Cornell University Medical College: Lasdon Facility Budget42Our Changing Campus43Nuclear Studies at Cornell University44Nuclear Studies at Cornell University45Cornell University Medical College: Lasdon Biomedical Research Center46Cornell University Medical College: Lasdon Biomedical Research Center47Cornell University Medical College: Lasdon Biomedical Research Center48Cornell University Medical College: Lasdon Biomedical Research Center49Cornell University Medical College: Lasdon Biomedical Research Center410Cornell University Medical College: Lasdon Biomedical Research Center411Cornell University Medical College: Lasdon Biomedical Research Center412Correspondence with F.R.
